# DiabeticNode

> The crystal-clear, hardware-by-hardware breakdown of OpenDiabetic's sovereign diabetic compute.
> Privacy-first by construction: private health data never leaves the box; models flow down, receipts
> flow up, PHI crosses never. It takes a swarm to run the node — the hive cooks, the vault holds, the
> edge runs, the wearables reach the diabetic. Honest status labels: LIVE / IN BUILD / FRAMEWORK.

## The three tiers
- The Hive (cook): RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell GPU fleet — 97GB VRAM/card (194GB/rig) to slow-cook 27B anchors on owned, sovereign silicon.
- The Vault (store): Synology NAS — the only place PHI lives at rest; a filing cabinet, not a cloud.
- The Edge (run): Jetson Orin edge brain runs the model on-box (RAM-only PHI, receipt-only writes), plus the consumer device swarm.

## The device swarm (use-cases)
- Apple Watch: the generic nudge on your wrist (foot-check, meds, appointments) — never a record.
- iPhone: the interface/window to the on-box brain — not a PHI store.
- CGM & IoT sensors (Dexcom, foot-temp mats): readings reasoned over locally on the box.
- Home Advisor: in-home voice helper, answered on-box.
- Whisper: on-device speech-to-text; audio never leaves the box.

## Pages
- [Overview](https://diabeticnode.com/): privacy-first compute, the three tiers, the swarm, the flow.
- [The Fleet](https://diabeticnode.com/the-fleet): why Blackwell GPUs, why Synology storage, why sovereign.
- [The Edge](https://diabeticnode.com/the-edge): why Jetson Orin, and the consumer device swarm use-cases.

## The house
- OpenDiabetic (the foundation): https://opendiabetic.com
- DiabeticDocs (the full platform docs): https://diabeticdocs.com
